title: Source lineage for CME00047
summary: CME00047 is the repository XML representation of Joseph Thomas Fowler's 1899 Surtees Society volume II, an edited calendar and selection from eight Durham priory documentary groups rather than one medieval work or manuscript. The XML includes the edition title page and printed pp. 285–576. Current official catalogues establish 351 distinct public IIIF objects (1,233 canvases), 85 exact office-account entries without IIIF, eleven stock/livestock records without IIIF, and DCD Misc.Ch. 7269. Every public object was cached separately as a complete provider-published inventory where supported (235) or partial physical-object inventory (116), with exact Fowler citations and canvas locators. The twenty-five Marescalcia source identities and the full many-to-many item crosswalk remain unresolved.
review_notes: Do not treat Fowler's 399 editorial containers, current catalogue groups, IIIF manifests, duplicate copies, or physical rolls as interchangeable counts.
Complete for generic-label IIIF bundles means every provider-published canvas, not independent proof that every physical side was photographed.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00047
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00047.
TEMPHEAD's 2016 review history describes a later proofing and structural pass, not the original keying date.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00047
entities.description: Public electronic text encoding Fowler volume II's title page and printed pp. 285–576. It omits the Surtees Society preliminaries, council resolution, Fowler's preface, contents, and later society matter.

entities.label: Joseph Thomas Fowler, Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, volume II (1899)
entities.description: Surtees Society volume 100. A calendar and selective edition of eight groups of Durham priory records, not a diplomatic edition of one manuscript. Fowler notes that the Bursar material in particular required severe selection.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Thomas Fowler, ed., Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, from the Original MSS., vol. II, Publications of the Surtees Society 100 (Durham: Andrews & Co. for the Society, 1899).
bibliographic.title: Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, from the Original MSS.
bibliographic.publisher: Andrews & Co. for the Surtees Society

physical_description.extent: Eight preliminary pages and 292 continuously paginated text pages, represented in a 336-page access scan
physical_description.format: Printed scholarly calendar and edited documentary collection
notes: The series title labels volume 100 as the publication for the Society's year 1898, while the edition title page and imprint are dated 1899.

entities.label: Joseph Thomas Fowler, Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, volume I (1898)
entities.description: The preceding volume. Its preface gives Fowler's general method: a few rolls in full, selected interesting or important details from the others, and at least a mention of every then-known roll.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Thomas Fowler, ed., Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, from the Original MSS., vol. I, Publications of the Surtees Society 99 (Durham: Andrews & Co. for the Society, 1898).
bibliographic.title: Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, from the Original MSS.
bibliographic.publisher: Andrews & Co. for the Surtees Society

entities.label: Joseph Thomas Fowler, Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, volume III (1901)
entities.description: The concluding volume. Its retrospective introduction describes the offices, archive, editorial project, and relative scale of the Bursar extracts.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Thomas Fowler, ed., Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, from the Original MSS., vol. III, Publications of the Surtees Society 103 (Durham: Andrews & Co. for the Society, 1901).
bibliographic.title: Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, from the Original MSS.
bibliographic.publisher: Andrews & Co. for the Surtees Society

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Fowler volume II
entities.description: A 336-page access PDF retaining the provider's complete exposed bound-volume sequence. The CME selection is edition title page at leaf 11 and printed pp. 285–576 at leaves 17–308.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Fowler volume I
entities.description: Complete scan retained as evidence for Fowler's general editorial method.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Fowler volume III
entities.description: Complete scan retained for the retrospective introduction and context for the three-volume project.

entities.label: Durham Cathedral Archive: Medieval Accounting Material—office holders (obedientiaries)
entities.description: Current official item-level catalogue for the Communar, Terrar, Sacrist, Feretrar, Bursar, and related office-account series. Its SS100 references create a many-to-many crosswalk to Fowler's editorial units.

entities.label: Durham Cathedral Archive: Medieval Accounting Material—manorial and subsidiary accounts
entities.description: Current official catalogue identifying eleven stock-keeper, livestock, and Holme accounts cited to Fowler volume II pp. 309–322.

entities.label: Durham Cathedral Archive: Miscellaneous Charters 6000–7309
entities.description: Current official catalogue identifying DCD Misc.Ch. 7269 as the account of Henry Helay, supervisor equicii, printed by Fowler on pp. 323–325.

entities.label: Durham Archives and Cathedral reproduction and publication policies
entities.description: Official ordering, researcher-photography, and publication-licensing guidance. The policy files were discoverable but returned HTTP 403 to anonymous scripted download during this review, so no local copy is claimed.

holding.notes: Publication permission is administered by Durham Cathedral Library.

notes: DCD-Bur.acs Sale of Tithes, [1316 ?] [1316 ?] ...xvj The figures are only visible by ultra-violet light, and it cannot be certain that there was only one "x". Given that the date appears to be "16", the dating of the Sale of Tithes, 1292-3 would suggest that this would be taken as 1316-7, but the details given on the Bursar's Accounts 1316-7 and 1317-8 do not tally with those given on this roll. It is therefore possible that the date of this roll is 1315-6, but there seems to be no means of testing this. Mentioned: Surtees Society 100. 520. (c. 1330. Two). Index terms Durham Priory. Bursar Durham Priory -- AccountingAccount books
No public facsimile was linked from the exact official entry on 2026-07-12.

notes: DCD-Sacr. acs [1322-4] [1322-4] ... [5 August 1322 - 1324] ... [John of Darlington] The expenses are headed as being from Oswald '22. The account covers two harvests; there are payments of stipends for both eighteen months and two years. The last date mentioned appears to be the beginning of Lent following the harvest of '23, ie 29 February 1324. John of Darlington occurs as Sacrist 12 August 1322 [Misc.Ch.7031]. Extracted: Surtees Society 100. 375.

notes: DCD-Sacr. acs [1341] [1341] ... ... [Thomas Graystanes]Dorse: Status The latest arrears mentioned in the balance are for the Martinmas term '41 and the account cannot have been closed before that. It is not clear that it extended into 1342, although payments made at Christmas are included; a different account, opened on 22 April 1342, was preceded by an Account where the total of arrears given is so small as to preclude identification with this Account, for which no more precise closing date can be given than between Christmas 1341 and 22 April 1342. There is no evidence for the opening date of the Account. Accountant named on Bursar's Accounts 1341-2 and on subsequent Sacrists' Accounts. Mentioned: Surtees Society 100. 378.
No public facsimile was linked from the exact official entry on 2026-07-12.

notes: DCD-Sacr. acs 1358-9 1358-9 ... ...Dorse: Status The form of this account follows that introduced after the account for 1355-6 on the account of 1356-7; it does not duplicate this latter. On the other hand the practice of adding a Status to the account had been dropped before the accounts for 1359-60, but is still found in 1356-7. It may therefore be concluded that this account is for one of the two years between 1357 and 1359. The inclusion of a receipt in respect of Bedlington Church indicates 1358-9: the Bursar received this in 1357, but his account for 1358-9 records its assignment to the Sacrist. Extracted: Surtees Society 100. 381 "c. 1350".
No public facsimile was linked from the exact official entry on 2026-07-12.

notes: DCD-Enr. lstk. acs [c.26 May ? 1340] - Shearing [c.26 May ?] 1341 [c.26 May ? 1340] - Shearing [c.26 May ?] 1341 View at marking and shearing 1341General heading. The evidence for interpreting the heading is the same as that for the roll for 1339-40. Some at least of the accounts on this roll follow on immediately from those on the roll for 1339-40, since they start by taking up the numbers of stock given as remaining at the end of accounts on the roll for 1339-40; why there should be some other accounts here, such as Ketton, in which this does not apparently happen is not clear. Face m 1HolmeJohn Page bercarius of Holme BelasisJohn bercarius of Belasis BewleyJohn bercarius Barton Cot esStephen bercarius Ketton (muttons)Adam bercarius of Ketton KettonRobert bercarius of Ketton PittingtonJohn Yong' bercarius m 2Muggleswick horsesWilliam Couhird stodardus MuggleswickWilliam Couhird vaccarius No indication of place is given. Entries for transfers of cattle between Bearpark and Muggleswick found in the Bearpark account below match entries in this account. Waskerleyhead (stirks)William Cutt es In the Muggleswick account above entries for transfers of cattle refer to William Cuttes as 'bovarius'. The single section concerns younger stirks; other categories of cattle were perhaps in the hands of the following accountant, see below. ? Waskerleyhead(cattle) John Walkere This account has no marginal heading giving the place. It follows the Waskerleyhead account above with less of a gap
No public facsimile was linked from the exact official entry on 2026-07-12.

entities.label: DCD-Enr. lstk. acs 1380-1
entities.description: An exact current Durham catalogue record cross-referenced to Fowler volume II. No linked public IIIF object was exposed on the review date; physical-copy and surface-level facsimile coverage therefore remain unavailable.

holding.notes: Publication permission is administered by Durham Cathedral Library.

notes: DCD-Enr. lstk. acs 1380-1 1380-1 Four rolls, apparently originally forming a single file, to judge by the corresponding pairs of holes at the head of each roll, through which a filing thong could have passed. The rolls were probably separate by the early sixteenth century, when Thomas Swalwell added a different date on the dorse of each of them: '1380' on (i), '1381' on (ii), '1382' on (iii), and '1383' on (iv). In doing this doubtless he followed the headings on two of the rolls: 'Augustine ... 80 - same feast 81' on (i), and 'Augustine 1381 - Katherine [25 November] following' on (ii). The basis for his dating of (iii) and (iv) is not clear; both are headed 'Adhuc', indicating that they continued from preceding rolls, and (iii) has an uncompleted heading ending 'A festo sancti', while the last account on (iv) is headed 'Augustine 1380 - same feast following'. Although there are twenty-one separate accounts no two accounts cover the same category of stock at the same place, and so there is no prima facie evidence that the accounts cover entirely different periods. In fact there is clear evidence that accounts on each roll cover at least part of a single period: movements of animals between one centre and another recorded in an account on one roll can be traced in a number of cases in the account for the other centre involved on one of the other rolls. Thus the movements of muttons recorded in the Merrington account on roll (iv) are matched by corresponding entries in the acc
No public facsimile was linked from the exact official entry on 2026-07-12.

entities.label: DCD-Enr. lstk. acs 1387-8
entities.description: An exact current Durham catalogue record cross-referenced to Fowler volume II. No linked public IIIF object was exposed on the review date; physical-copy and surface-level facsimile coverage therefore remain unavailable.

holding.notes: Publication permission is administered by Durham Cathedral Library.

notes: DCD-Enr. lstk. acs 1387-8 1387-8 Face m 1Martin [11 November] 1387 - Martin 1388General heading. Only four of the accounts on this roll have headings giving the dating of an individual account; they all differ in dating from the general heading and cover shorter periods. There are no good grounds for thinking that the general heading does not apply to all the other accounts. Although some of them state that payment of the accountant's stipend was up to Invention of Cross, 3 May, only, one of these is the first account which is explicitly headed 'de tempore supradicte'. Several of them also refer to Robert of Crayke as lately cellarer and William of Kelloe as cellarer now; Robert accounted up to 21 August 1388 and William from 18 September 1388. For said periodRobert Steuenson vaccarius at Carpshiel Thomas Johnson vaccarius at Easter Blackdene in Weardale m 2Walter Colson vaccarius at Muggleswick Martin [11 November] '87 - Invention of Cross following [3 May 1388]Walter Todd stirkettarius at Wharnley Invention of Cross [3 May] '88 - Martin following [11 November 1388]John Collauwode stirkettarius at Burnhopeshiel in Weardale William Jakson stirkettarius at 'Burnhamschele' John Brak vaccarius at Waskerleyhead m 3John Gryce bercarius at 'Pollowhill' Adam de Fourneys bercarius at the Westgate Peter Scathelok bovettarius at Waskerleyhead Conversion of Paul 1387 [25 January 1388] -Martin [11 November] 1388Emina widow of Thomas bercar' of the Hope (at Westoe) m 4Richard White vaccar
No public facsimile was linked from the exact official entry on 2026-07-12.

notes: DCD-Enr. lstk. acs 1398-1400 1398-1400 Face m 1Martin [11 November] '399 - Martin [11 November] 1400General heading. The headings to nine of the accounts that follow give no dates, but refer back, presumably to the heading in the case of the first eight accounts. William Sawer's second account is headed for exactly the same period and there are entries corresponding to liveries in that account in the first four accounts below. For said -Robert Cragg vaccarius at Overdeyhouse For said periodJohn Raven vaccarius at Netherdayhouse For said periodRichard Robinson vaccarius at Whitehall For said periodWalter Todd vaccarius at Waskerleyhcad m 2For said periodWllliam del Shele stirkettarius at Birkhouse For said periodRobert Jonson stottarius at Waskerleyhead For said periodJohn Mayson bercarius at Birkhouse For said periodThomas bercarius at Bearpark m 2Eight weeks after Pentecost - Martin [c.1 August ? - 11 November 1400]John Wethirhard hoggettarius at Houghall The opening date reads ' per te a feste a festo Pentecostes post per viij septimanas vsque ...'. The first attempt simply follows the wording the previous accounts, 'per tempus predictum' The interpretation of what now stands is adopted because the account cannot cover as little as eight weeks, nor can the period between Pentecost whenever it falls, and Martin amount to either eighteen or twenty-eight weeks. The year is not given. The number of lambs received from Westoe is the same as that entered below in the Westoe accou
No public facsimile was linked from the exact official entry on 2026-07-12.

notes: DCD Misc.Ch. 7269 1420's Account of surveyor of the prior of Durham's horses, Henry Helay supervisor equicii prioris, Chains of St Peter [1 Aug.] 1420 - same feast [1421, and thence] - Invention of Cross next following [3 May 1422]. It is clear the second closing date in the heading was intended to extend the period covered by the account, rather than reduce it, from the fact that payments for agistment are for the period Invention of Cross - Michaelmas. Printed: SS 100, 323-5.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A large archive of separate accounts, duplicate copies, fragments, schedules, and related records
editorial_practices.representation: A few records printed in full, selected particulars extracted from many, and other sources calendared or mentioned
editorial_practices.description: Fowler's volume I preface states the project's general method; volume II's preface emphasizes the especially severe selection required for the Bursar series.
editorial_practices.scope: All eight sections, especially the Bursar accounts

editorial_practices.source_feature: Separated pieces, duplicates, drafts, and related records that have sometimes been rejoined or reclassified in the modern catalogue
editorial_practices.representation: Edition headings and excerpts arranged as a historical calendar
editorial_practices.description: Current catalogue notes show that modern physical grouping and dating do not always match Fowler's editorial units. Relations therefore retain exact current entry and ARK mappings rather than forcing one DIV2 to one roll.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A complete printed volume with society preliminaries, council resolution, preface, contents, title page, text, and back matter
editorial_practices.representation: Edition title page and printed pp. 285–576 encoded; other matter omitted
editorial_practices.description: The electronic item is narrower than the complete bound-volume scan even though its eight body sections span all continuously paginated text in volume II.

editorial_practices.representation: TEMPHEAD records a later proofing and structural review
editorial_practices.description: The review note does not supply an original keying date or complete transcription-method history.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00047.xml.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD; IDG; FRONT; BODY/DIV1, DIV2, and DIV3; PB milestones
evidence.summary: Supplies exact repository provenance, identifiers, encoded title page, eight sections, 399 DIV2 editorial units, language declaration, text, notes, and printed-page milestones.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00047 item record and full text.
evidence.summary: Confirms the public electronic item, title, inherited Durham Cathedral corporate-author heading, 1899 publication, complete electronic contents, public-domain statement, and No Copyright–United States URI.

evidence.citation: Joseph Thomas Fowler, ed., Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, vol. II (Surtees Society 100, 1899), complete scan.
evidence.locator: Title page; society matter; preface; contents; printed pp. 285–576
evidence.summary: Confirms title, imprint, editor, date, eight section titles and page ranges, source dates, item texts, notes, and physical descriptions.

evidence.citation: Fowler, preface to Extracts from the Account Rolls, vol. II.
evidence.summary: Explains the difficulty of selecting from the massive Bursar material, states that volume II ends with 1370–71, and announces volume III's continuation, introduction, glossary, and index.

evidence.citation: Joseph Thomas Fowler, ed., Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, vol. I (1898), complete scan.
evidence.summary: Retained as supporting evidence for the general editorial method stated in volume I's preface.

evidence.citation: Fowler, preface to Extracts from the Account Rolls, vol. I, pp. vii–viii.
evidence.locator: Preface, especially the selection and calendar statements
evidence.summary: States that only a few rolls are printed in full, selected interesting or important particulars are extracted from others, and no then-known roll is left without some mention.

evidence.citation: Joseph Thomas Fowler, ed., Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, vol. III (1901), complete scan.
evidence.summary: Retained for institutional descriptions and retrospective evidence about the three-volume project.

evidence.citation: Fowler, introduction to Extracts from the Account Rolls, vol. III, pp. i–lxvii.
evidence.locator: Especially office descriptions and the retrospective editorial account
evidence.summary: Describes Durham's offices and records, the archive's institutional creators, and the relative scale and selective character of the Bursar extracts.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for extractsaccountr02surtuoft.
evidence.summary: Identifies the volume II item, Robarts Library source, 2006 scan, exact files, and NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT status in the United States.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive page-number map for extractsaccountr02surtuoft.
evidence.summary: Maps the edition title page to leaf 11 and printed pp. 285–576 to leaves 17–308 in the 336-page PDF sequence.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for extractsfromacco01durh.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete volume I supporting scan and files; no separate scan-reuse licence is supplied.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive page-number map for extractsfromacco01durh.
evidence.summary: Maps the complete supporting volume I scan.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for extractsfromacco00durh.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete volume III supporting scan and files; no separate scan-reuse licence is supplied.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive page-number map for extractsfromacco00durh.
evidence.summary: Maps the complete supporting volume III scan and its retrospective introduction.

evidence.citation: Durham University Library, Catalogue of Medieval Accounting Material in the Durham Cathedral Archive: office holders (obedientiaries).
evidence.locator: SS100-linked Communar, Terrar, Sacrist, Feretrar, Bursar, and cross-reference entries
evidence.summary: Supplies current shelfmarks, duplicate-copy distinctions, dates, descriptions, SS100 citations, exact IIIF links, custody, and publication-permission requirements.

evidence.citation: Durham University Library, Catalogue of Medieval Accounting Material: manorial and subsidiary accounts.
evidence.locator: Eleven exact SS100-linked stock and livestock entries
evidence.summary: Identifies eleven current archival records excerpted on Fowler pp. 309–322; no public IIIF link is exposed for these entries.

evidence.citation: Durham University Library, Durham Cathedral Archive Miscellaneous Charters 6000–7309.
evidence.summary: Identifies Henry Helay's 1420–1422 supervisor equicii account as the source printed on Fowler pp. 323–325; no public IIIF link is exposed.

evidence.citation: Direct audit of the 351 exact Durham IIIF Presentation 2 manifests linked from SS100-cross-referenced catalogue entries.
evidence.summary: Verified and cached every public SS100-linked object separately at requested width 1800,. All manifests state CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. Coverage is 235 complete provider-published inventories and 116 partial physical-object inventories; generic numeric labels are not independent proof that every side was photographed.
notes: Every ZIP checksum, byte count, canvas label, source URL, exact Fowler citation, and work portion is recorded in the corresponding access entry.

evidence.citation: Durham University Library, researcher photography policy for Archives and Special Collections.
evidence.summary: States that researcher images are for personal study or noncommercial research and may not be distributed online or in print without separate permission; complete-file photography may be restricted for preservation.
notes: Anonymous scripted retrieval returned HTTP 403; no local copy is claimed.

evidence.citation: Durham University Library, Ordering Copies—Services and Charges (2025 onwards).
evidence.summary: Provides priced research-image and higher-resolution ordering routes, with larger orders requiring enquiry.
notes: Anonymous scripted retrieval returned HTTP 403; no local copy is claimed.

evidence.citation: Durham University Library, Licensing Fees (April 2024 onwards), together with the Cathedral Archive publication statement.
evidence.summary: Provides publication-licensing categories and reinforces the need to obtain written permission for publication, web, commercial, or other reuse.
notes: Anonymous scripted retrieval returned HTTP 403; no local copy is claimed.

open_questions.question: How do Fowler's 399 XML DIV2 editorial units map many-to-many onto current physical records, duplicate copies, fragments, schedules, rejoined pieces, and grouped catalogue entries?
next_steps: Build a page-by-page crosswalk keyed by part, Fowler heading, printed page, current shelfmark, duplicate letter, and IIIF ARK.
Do not force one XML unit to one physical object.

open_questions.question: What are the current shelfmarks, survival states, and reproduction routes for the twenty-five Marescalcia Prioris editorial records on Fowler pp. 326–371?
next_steps: Ask Durham Cathedral Library to crosswalk each Fowler heading and date to current archival references.
Keep each physical roll or record separate once identified; do not invent one aggregate Marescalcia manuscript.

open_questions.question: Can complete physical-object facsimiles be obtained for the 85 exact SS100-linked office-account catalogue entries without IIIF, eleven stock/livestock records, and DCD Misc.Ch. 7269?
next_steps: Ask whether complete images already exist for each exact shelfmark.
Request every physical surface, membrane, schedule, attachment, and contextual view; map the Fowler passage separately.

open_questions.question: Do fuller surrogates exist for the 116 public IIIF objects whose published manifests expose fewer reverse surfaces than faces?
next_steps: Send Durham the exact ARK list identified by access entries with partial local-copy coverage.
Ask whether omitted sides are blank, inaccessible, or merely absent from the public manifest.

open_questions.question: Do generic numeric, lettered, schedule, or catalogue-anchor canvas labels in 151 nominally complete bundles represent every physical surface and each distinct copy?
next_steps: Ask Durham to confirm physical-side and duplicate-copy semantics for generic-label manifests, especially DCD-Bur.acs 1316-7 ARK t2m41687j11b with canvases a and b.
Treat complete as provider-inventory completeness unless explicit face/dorse evidence supports a stronger claim.

open_questions.question: How should the exact item manifests' CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 licence be reconciled with the catalogue's separate requirement for advance permission for any published use?
next_steps: Obtain written clarification before placing images in Git, a public viewer, a print-on-demand product, or another commercial or adapted publication.
Keep the present cache private and Git-ignored meanwhile.

open_questions.question: When and by what item-specific process was CME00047 first keyed before its recorded 2016 review?
next_steps: Consult U-M collection production records or archived item metadata.
Do not equate the collection's 2016–2018 review batch with first encoding without item-level evidence.
